channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, threatening peace talks to end war
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged heated rhetoric and airstrikes in the region over the weekend, threatening a fragile ceasefire and ongoing negotiations for a permanent end to the war.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Iran exchanged heated rhetoric and airstrikes in the region over the weekend, threatening a fragile ceasefire and ongoing negotiations for a permanent end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Former Iran nuclear deal negotiator on what U.S.-Iran 'new normal' looks like
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Record-breaking heatwave moves east, engulfing more of Europe
item.description: Europe's deadly, record-breaking heatwave is pushing east, bringing extreme temperatures from Rome to Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: Europe's deadly, record-breaking heatwave is pushing east, bringing extreme temperatures from Rome to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
item.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
item.content:encoded:
A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

(Image credit: Valar Atomics)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
item.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.content:encoded: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes over the weekend, the search for survivors continues after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, Trump's immigration agenda gets boost from SCOTUS.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes over the weekend, the search for survivors continues after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, Trump's immigration agenda gets boost from SCOTUS.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: PJ Morton showcases pop and gospel influences on new album
item.description: A member of the pop band Maroon 5, PJ Morton hasn't forgotten his roots in gospel music. He splits his influences down the middle on the new double-album, "Saturday Night, Sunday Morning."
item.content:encoded: A member of the pop band Maroon 5, PJ Morton hasn't forgotten his roots in gospel music. He splits his influences down the middle on the new double-album, "Saturday Night, Sunday Morning."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: World Cup enters knockout phase
item.description: The World Cup continues to thrill as the tournament has entered the knockout phase. No longer do goal differential or standings in the group stage matter. From now on, it's win or go home.
item.content:encoded: The World Cup continues to thrill as the tournament has entered the knockout phase. No longer do goal differential or standings in the group stage matter. From now on, it's win or go home.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: Drought conditions in several Western states force farmers to make tough decisions
item.description: A historically dry winter in many Western states has ranchers facing tough choices.
item.content:encoded: A historically dry winter in many Western states has ranchers facing tough choices.
item.dc:creator: Dan Boyce

item.title: Search for survivors continues after devastating earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: Search and rescue efforts continue across Venezuela as worried families report nearly 70,000 people as missing.
item.content:encoded: Search and rescue efforts continue across Venezuela as worried families report nearly 70,000 people as missing.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta
